evidence: Package decomposition (grammar, query, indexer, api, config, tagging, utils, viz) maps recognisably to domain capabilities — a new engineer can name each package in one phrase.

CLAUDE.md lists ~10 classes that do not exist in the source tree (HybridLuceneIndexer, CollocationsBuilderV2, HybridIndex, HybridQueryExecutor, WordSketchQueryExecutor, SnowballCollocations, ConllUProcessor, CQLToLuceneCompiler, LuceneIndexer). The code-structure diagram in the project's primary reference doc is entirely wrong.

WordSketchApiServer.java is 1106 lines containing route registration, request parsing, response serialisation, and business logic for 10+ endpoints, a legacy RelationDef inner class, and a toLegacyRelationType() bridge method — a clear God Class whose change surface is the entire API.

SketchGrammarParser (grammar package) parses .wsdef.m4 format files and is never called anywhere in production code; runtime grammar loading is handled solely by GrammarConfigLoader from JSON. The class exists and compiles but is dead weight that implies a capability the system does not have.

.bak files (GrammarPatternCollocationsBuilder.java.bak, GrammarPatternCollocationsReader.java.bak) sit inside src/main/java/.../indexer/hybrid/ — committed dead code inside the production source tree.

QueryExecutor interface embeds a RelationType enum (ADJ_MODIFIER, ADJ_PREDICATE, SUBJECT_OF, OBJECT_OF) alongside GrammarConfigLoader's string-based relationType field (SURFACE, DEP). WordSketchApiServer bridges them with toLegacyRelationType(), meaning a new engineer must understand two parallel systems to trace any relation through the stack.

CQLVerifier and the entire predicate hierarchy live in the query package but are never invoked on the production code path — BlackLabQueryExecutor delegates to BlackLab's own CQL parser. Their inclusion in query alongside live production classes blurs the boundary between runtime and test-only infrastructure.

handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti inlines its own multi-seed intersection logic rather than delegating to SemanticFieldExplorer, while handleSemanticField calls SemanticFieldExplorer.compare() for the same conceptual operation.
high_level_elegance.issues_preventing_higher_score: CLAUDE.md is the primary reference document and lists ~10 non-existent classes; the God Class API server concentrates change risk; a legacy predicate subsystem and orphaned parser create false signals about system capabilities; dual relation-type representations force bridge code that only the original author can navigate.

evidence: AbstractPatternPredicate establishes a clean template method: extractValue() is the variation point, wildcard-to-regex lives in the base, 4 leaf classes reduce to one-liners.

Token.java carries matchesTag(), matchesLemma(), matchesWord(), matchesPosGroup() plus its own private matchesPattern(). A grep of all callers in src/main/java shows ZERO call sites for any of these methods — Token is pure data storage in practice, but ships 40+ lines of dead matching code.

Token.matchesPattern and AbstractPatternPredicate.wildcardToRegex diverge: Token handles bracket character classes while AbstractPatternPredicate escapes special chars differently — a latent correctness hazard.

PosTagger interface is declared with tagSentence/tagSentences/getName/getTagset, but SimpleTagger is its only implementation and all call sites use the concrete type. CLAUDE.md mentions UDPipeTagger but it does not exist.
QueryExecutor.RelationType enum lives inside the interface and carries literal BCQL/CQL pattern strings — leaking a specific query language syntax into the interface boundary.

NotPredicate is a dedicated class while TokenPredicate already provides a default negate() method. CQLVerifier exclusively uses new NotPredicate(...) rather than pred.negate(), making negate() dead API surface.
PatternSubstitution is a static utility class called from exactly one site. Its methods are public but have no other consumers.
The composite predicates (AndPredicate, OrPredicate, NotPredicate) each implement TokenPredicate directly as separate classes rather than factory methods on the @FunctionalInterface.
LogDiceCalculator bundles FrequencyAggregator and CollocationResult as nested static classes — genuinely earns its grouping.
abstraction_fitness.issues_preventing_higher_score: Token carries dead wildcard-matching methods that diverge from AbstractPatternPredicate's implementation; PosTagger interface adds indirection with zero active variation; RelationType embedding CQL pattern strings in the interface boundary is a real abstraction leak.

evidence: SemanticFieldExplorer.exploreByPattern (lines 91-265, ~175 lines) and explore (lines 268-416, ~148 lines) implement identical 4-step algorithm; steps 3-4 are copy-pasted verbatim
CQLParser: splitByOrOutsideQuotes and splitByAnd are structurally identical — both walk a string tracking inQuote state and split on a delimiter; differ only in the delimiter character

GrammarConfigLoader: deriveHeadPositionFromPattern and deriveCollocatePositionFromPattern are near-identical — same whitespace-skip, token-counting, bracket-matching logic, differ only in label digit '1:' vs '2:'

BlackLabQueryExecutor: the 'group hits → filter → compute logDice → build WordSketchResult list → sort/limit' pattern is duplicated across findCollocations, findDependencyCollocations, executeDependencyPattern, and executeSurfacePattern

Dead code in CQLParser.buildElement: lemmaMatcher extracts subPos/subField, modifies target via replaceFirst, then falls through; the comment 'will be used during compilation' indicates incomplete implementation
Bug: SemanticFieldExplorer.compare line 461: logger.info('  Top 5: {}', adjectives.stream()) — streams the Java Stream object reference into the logger, not the list contents

Double query execution in BlackLabQueryExecutor.executeDependencyPattern: blackLabIndex.find(query) fetches hits for the null-size guard, then blackLabIndex.search().find(query) re-executes the same query for grouping
executeBcqlQuery is a 130-line method with an inline record definition, two passes, and complex nested null-chains
GrammarConfigLoader constructor spans ~120 lines: JSON parsing, field validation, copula checks, pattern derivation, position counting, token validation, and map building all inline
Misspelling: REPITITION constant (CQLParser line 38) — used consistently but is a permanent naming defect
posGroupFromConstraint in RelationConfig uses 14 sequential if-contains chains for xpos/tag × POS variants instead of a lookup table
low_level_elegance.issues_preventing_higher_score: Pervasive method-level duplication across all four seed files is the primary obstacle. exploreByPattern/explore duplication alone spans ~150 lines. The four-file pattern of identical 'compute logDice from grouped hits' logic indicates a systemic copy-paste habit. Several individual methods exceed the one-glance threshold. Additionally there is a functional bug (stream-to-logger) and dead code (lemma substitution).

evidence: WordSketchApiServer has a full 5-method Builder (inner static class) for a 4-field object that is constructed in exactly one place — classic AI-generated scaffolding
RelationDef inner static class defined at line 1091 in WordSketchApiServer — never instantiated, pure dead class
toLegacyRelationType() private method in WordSketchApiServer defined but never invoked anywhere in the file

SemanticFieldExplorer.explore(String,int,int,int,double) and explore(String) methods (~150 lines of algorithm) duplicate the logic of exploreByPattern() with minor variable name changes; neither is ever called from outside the class
Identical relation-ID mapping switch appears verbatim in both handleSemanticFieldExplore() and handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti()
GrammarConfigLoader retains copulas/copulaSet fields (both always empty), getCopulas() returning empty list, isCopularVerb() always returning false
GrammarConfigLoader.getRelationsForHeadPos() and findRelationIdByType() are public methods defined but never called outside the class
RelationConfig.isLegacyFormat() defined but never called
QueryExecutor interface defines findGrammaticalRelation() default and isReady() default — neither called in any production path
LogDiceCalculator ships CollocationResult, FrequencyAggregator, computeMI3, computeTScore, computeLogLikelihood — only exercised by unit tests, never used in production code
Token class has its own matchesTag/matchesLemma/matchesWord/matchesPosGroup methods implementing the same pattern-matching logic as the parallel predicate classes
Verbose Javadoc on trivial single-line getters: getCopulas(), isCopularVerb(), getVersion(), getConfigPath(), getExecutorType(), isReady()
ConcordanceResult has 4 constructor overloads with Javadoc labels like 'backward compatibility', 'BlackLab-style', 'full constructor' — incremental AI-generated constructors never consolidated
34 .bak files in src/ tree for deleted production classes — the old implementation is stored inside the source tree rather than removed via git

CLAUDE.md code-structure diagram still lists CQLToLuceneCompiler.java, CollocationsBuilderV2.java, HybridIndex.java, SnowballCollocations.java, ConllUProcessor.java — none of which exist as real .java files any more
CLAUDE.md 'Key Components' section links to 'query/CQLToLuceneCompiler.java' (broken path) and lists 'tagging/UDPipeTagger.java' which is not present in source tree

QueryExecutor interface comment says 'Allows different implementations (legacy, hybrid, BlackLab) to be used interchangeably', and RelationType enum carries full CQL/Lucene patterns never used by the only real implementation
WordSketchApiServer.toLegacyRelationType() is defined but never called — dead code bridging the old RelationType enum
QueryResults.java Javadoc says 'Extracted from WordSketchQueryExecutor for reuse' — referring to a class that now exists only as a .bak file
CQLVerifier, CQLParser, CQLPattern, SketchGrammarParser are live .java files used only by tests or not at all from production paths
GrammarConfigLoader.RelationConfig.isLegacyFormat() is defined but never called
CLAUDE.md still references 'collocations.bin (700MB)', 'CollocationsBuilderV2 NUM_WORKERS' tuning — referring to the old hybrid pipeline replaced by BlackLab

query package contains 19 classes spanning token domain model (Token, TokenWindow), a 9-class predicate hierarchy (TokenPredicate, AbstractPatternPredicate, LemmaPredicate, TagPredicate, WordPredicate, PosGroupPredicate, AndPredicate, OrPredicate, NotPredicate), query executors (QueryExecutor, BlackLabQueryExecutor, BlackLabSnippetParser, CQLVerifier), result types (QueryResults), and high-level exploration (SemanticFieldExplorer)
LongIntHashMapTest is placed in pl.marcinmilkowski.word_sketch.query but tests LongIntHashMap which lives in pl.marcinmilkowski.word_sketch.utils — test does not mirror main package structure

RelationType enum (grammatical relations ADJ_MODIFIER, ADJ_PREDICATE, SUBJECT_OF, OBJECT_OF) is nested inside the QueryExecutor interface — a domain/grammar concept buried in an infrastructure interface
utils package contains two conceptually unrelated classes: LogDiceCalculator (corpus statistics domain concept) and LongIntHashMap (generic data structure infrastructure)
BlackLabSnippetParser has package-private visibility (class, not public class) — good internal hiding, but belongs to a different sub-concern than the predicate hierarchy sharing the same package

BlackLabQueryExecutor.findCollocations() swallows InvalidQuery with logger.warn + empty return, while findDependencyCollocations(), executeQuery(), executeBcqlQuery(), and executeSurfacePattern() all wrap InvalidQuery as IOException and re-throw — same exception, five different call sites, two different behaviors

GrammarConfigLoader exposes two parallel lookup methods: getRelation(String) returning Optional<RelationConfig> and getRelationById(String) returning nullable RelationConfig; WordSketchApiServer uses getRelationById at lines 344/447 and getRelation at lines 514/666/898 with no documented reason

SemanticFieldExplorer.close() always closes its executor regardless of constructor used; WordSketchApiServer creates explorers with new SemanticFieldExplorer(this.executor) (injected executor not owned) then calls explorer.close() — double-close on the shared BlackLabIndex
logger.debug() calls in WordSketchApiServer and PatternSubstitution embed redundant 'DEBUG:' prefix strings in the message text
Dead inner class RelationDef at bottom of WordSketchApiServer — fields and constructor defined, never instantiated
SemanticFieldExplorer instantiated fresh per HTTP request rather than being a server-level singleton
12 test files use *Test.java suffix but 2 files use Test* prefix: TestBCQLParser.java and TestGrammarConfig.java
RelationsValidationTest.java has no JUnit annotations and only a main() method — it is a standalone script named as a JUnit test
TestGrammarConfig.java is a test helper/factory class (no @Test methods) placed in the test package with 'Test' in its name
BlackLabConllUIndexer.java uses System.out/System.err for all progress and error reporting (10+ calls) with no SLF4J logger, while all other non-trivial classes use private static final Logger
7 main-source files use 'import java.util.*' wildcard while the majority use fully-qualified specific imports
BlackLabSnippetParser.java is package-private (no public modifier on the class declaration) while every other main-source class is public
README documents /api/grammar/active endpoint that is never registered in WordSketchApiServer.java
README shows ?pos, ?limit, and ?scoring query parameters for /api/sketch/{lemma} but handleFullSketch() ignores all query params

README concordance response shows highlighted field with <mark> tags and word1_positions/word2_positions arrays, but code sets exMap.put("highlighted", r.getSentence()) (plain text) and never adds position arrays
HttpApiUtils.sendError() does not set Access-Control-Allow-Origin header, causing CORS failures for browser clients receiving error responses
handleBcqlQueryPost() catch block calls sendJsonResponse() returning HTTP 200 with {"error": ...} instead of using sendError() with appropriate 4xx/5xx code
handleSemanticField() edge objects use key 'logDice'; handleSemanticFieldExplore() and handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti() edge objects use key 'weight' for the same semantic concept
Server startup log in code lists 11 endpoints; README startup-output snippet shows only 4
Private static class RelationDef (WordSketchApiServer lines 1091-1105) has fields id, name, description, deprel, showInUi but is never instantiated or used anywhere
Legacy relation alias switch (adj_predicate->noun_adj_predicates, etc.) is duplicated verbatim in both handleSemanticFieldExplore() and handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti()
BlackLabDummyTest.testSubjectOf() has zero assertions; swallows exceptions silently; just prints to stderr and returns if index missing
RelationsValidationTest uses public static void main() instead of @Test; Maven Surefire will never execute it
DependencySketchTest and BlackLabBcqlConcordanceTest hard-code Windows path D:\\corpora_philsci\\bi; all tests skip silently in CI via Assumptions.assumeTrue or @EnabledIf
WordSketchApiServer.java (REST API), SemanticFieldExplorer.java (key advertised feature), BlackLabSnippetParser.java, SketchGrammarParser.java have zero test coverage
CQLVerifierTest verifies only isMatched()==true for most cases; does not assert which token was matched, at what position, or what the matched lemma was
CQLParserTest has 30+ well-named cases covering labeled positions, negation, OR, AND, distance, regex-vs-field OR disambiguation
LogDiceCalculatorTest includes symmetry property test, monotonicity test, and real-corpus scenario regression tests
LongIntHashMapTest tests collision/rehash with small initial capacity, negative keys, Long.MAX_VALUE
TestGrammarConfig is a utility class with no @Test methods placed in test directory — creates noise

strengths: Package names (grammar, query, indexer, api, config, tagging, utils, viz) map directly to domain concepts — a newcomer can orient immediately.
QueryExecutor interface provides a clean seam between the API layer and query execution; WordSketchApiServer depends only on the interface, not the concrete BlackLabQueryExecutor.
GrammarConfigLoader externalises relation definitions to JSON rather than hard-coding them, making the configuration boundary explicit and changeable without touching source.
HttpApiUtils and PatternSubstitution are small, well-scoped utility extractions that correctly own their responsibilities.
WordSketchApiServer uses a Builder pattern for construction, keeping instantiation flexible and the dependencies visible.
high_level_elegance.issue_character: The problems are structural and systemic: the primary reference document misrepresents the codebase, a God Class concentrates all API responsibility, and several subsystems (predicate hierarchy, SketchGrammarParser, legacy RelationType enum) are either dead or parallel duplicates that survive from earlier architecture phases.
high_level_elegance.score_rationale: The package taxonomy earns credit for domain alignment and the QueryExecutor/GrammarConfigLoader seams are solid design decisions, placing this above a failing baseline. However, the CLAUDE.md structural diagram is completely wrong (violating the 'reference docs match runtime reality' criterion directly), the 1106-line God Class is the only entry point for all API concerns, and a dead predicate subsystem plus orphaned SketchGrammarParser create false impressions of system capabilities. This sits in the 55–60 range: recognisable structure with significant documentation/decomposition drag.

strengths: AbstractPatternPredicate template method is textbook: extractValue() is the single variation point, base class owns wildcard-to-regex, four leaf classes are one-liners.
QueryExecutor interface serves a real seam: SemanticFieldExplorer uses it as a mockable boundary in its test constructor, and the API server holds a QueryExecutor field.
LogDiceCalculator groups cohesive pure functions, FrequencyAggregator, and CollocationResult in a single class; the nesting is genuinely domain-meaningful.
TokenPredicate as @FunctionalInterface with a default negate() is idiomatic and enables lambda predicates without boilerplate.
BlackLabSnippetParser packages complex regex-based XML parsing into a package-private helper class.
abstraction_fitness.issue_character: The issues are isolated dead-code artifacts and single-implementation interfaces — not systemic design failures — but they represent concrete indirection costs (PosTagger, Token.matchesXxx) that earn no benefit and introduce a divergent-implementation risk.
abstraction_fitness.score_rationale: The core predicate hierarchy and QueryExecutor interface are well-designed and demonstrate real abstraction leverage. However, Token ships 40+ lines of dead wildcard-matching code that silently diverges from the active AbstractPatternPredicate implementation, PosTagger is a hollow interface never held as a declared type with no second implementor in the codebase, and RelationType embeds CQL pattern strings inside the interface boundary making it effectively leaky. Placing this around 67-68: the predicate and executor abstractions push toward 80, but the hollow/dead abstractions pull the score down a full tier.

strengths: findMatchingBracket is short, single-purpose, and intention-revealing with a clean early-return guard
Stream pipelines are used appropriately in result sorting and filtering (sorted + limit + toList, Collectors.joining, etc.)
Guard clauses for null/empty inputs are consistently applied at the top of public methods
Static Pattern compilation at class load time avoids repeated compile overhead
Result data classes (DiscoveredNoun, CoreAdjective, ExplorationResult) are well-structured with focused toString/factory methods
low_level_elegance.issue_character: The dominant issue is systematic copy-paste duplication: the same multi-step algorithm is cloned rather than extracted, producing pairs of near-identical methods and a recurring 'group-hits → logDice → build-list' pattern duplicated four times.
low_level_elegance.score_rationale: The codebase sits well below the midpoint on low-level elegance. While individual leaf functions are often clean and guard clauses are present, the core algorithmic methods across all four seed files suffer from 50-150 line duplications that a single refactoring session would eliminate. The dead lemma-substitution code, the functional stream-to-logger bug, and oversized constructors/methods further undermine the 'read in one glance' standard. This is consistent with a global anchor around 40-45: readable at the leaf level but repeatedly failing the extraction and duplication criteria at the method level.

strengths: Core algorithm logic in SemanticFieldExplorer and GrammarConfigLoader is well-structured and readable
HttpApiUtils is correctly extracted as a small, focused utility class
QueryExecutor interface uses Java records and default methods cleanly for the parts that are actually used
Predicate hierarchy (And/Or/Not/Lemma/Tag/PosGroup/Word) is properly factored and each class is compact
ai_generated_debt.issue_character: The codebase contains a consistent pattern of generated-but-never-pruned scaffolding: dead inner classes, duplicate algorithm variants, always-empty infrastructure, and trivially-documented one-liners that were written to complete a class skeleton rather than because they were needed.
ai_generated_debt.score_rationale: The production-critical paths are clean, but significant surface area — roughly 15-20% of the files reviewed — consists of unused methods, dead classes, and duplicate logic that exists only to make the code look 'complete'. The RelationDef dead class, the two identical relation-mapping switch blocks, the copula infrastructure that always returns empty, and the duplicate explore() algorithm are the clearest signs of AI authorship without sufficient review.

strengths: The migration to BlackLab has a clean single-implementation entry point: Main.java has no legacy command handlers left, only blacklab-index/blacklab-query/server
The QueryExecutor interface cleanly abstracts the executor so the API server is decoupled from the backend
Old files were at least renamed to .bak rather than left as compilable dead code, preventing them from participating in the build
incomplete_migration.issue_character: A large hybrid/Lucene implementation was replaced by BlackLab but the migration was left at 80%: 34 .bak files clutter the source tree, documentation still describes the deleted architecture, and dead code from the old pipeline was never cleaned up.
incomplete_migration.score_rationale: The migration succeeded at the runtime level — the application boots and queries correctly through BlackLab only — but the codebase is littered with unmistakable migration artifacts. Thirty-four .bak files sitting inside src/ are the most visible sign; CLAUDE.md lists six classes that exist only as .bak; dead methods like toLegacyRelationType and isLegacyFormat add confusion; and grammar/CQLParser + query/CQLVerifier + grammar/SketchGrammarParser live in production source with no production caller.

strengths: grammar package is tightly cohesive — CQLParser, CQLPattern, CQLParseException, SketchGrammarParser all relate to CQL/sketch grammar parsing
tagging package cleanly isolates POS tagging concerns (PosTagger interface + SimpleTagger implementation)
api package groups HTTP-serving concerns (WordSketchApiServer, HttpApiUtils, PatternSubstitution) without leaking internals
config package is singularly focused on loading grammar configuration
AbstractPatternPredicate correctly uses package-private visibility, preventing internal predicate details from leaking outside the package
package_organization.issue_character: The query package has grown into a catch-all blob mixing token model, predicate combinator hierarchy, result DTOs, query executors, and high-level exploration logic that should each occupy their own sub-package or be redistributed.
package_organization.score_rationale: Several leaf packages (grammar, tagging, api, config) are well-organized and cohesive, earning meaningful credit. However the query package — the largest and most complex part of the codebase at 2682 lines across 19 classes — conflates at least four distinct sub-domains (token model, predicate evaluation, query execution, semantic exploration). Additionally, LongIntHashMapTest is in the wrong test package (query instead of utils), and RelationType, a grammatical domain concept, is inappropriately embedded in the QueryExecutor interface. These structural deficiencies meaningfully reduce discoverability and changeability for a developer working in this area.

strengths: Consistent use of SLF4J Logger across all non-CLI classes with static final field declaration idiom
Builder pattern applied uniformly to WordSketchApiServer construction
try-with-resources used consistently for BlackLabIndex and file I/O resource management
Consistent use of Collections.emptyList() / early-return-empty for null/missing-input guard clauses
QueryExecutor interface provides a clean seam with documented contracts and appropriate default methods
design_coherence.issue_character: Inconsistency introduced by incremental development: the same problem (lookup failure, query parse error, resource ownership) is solved differently depending on which method was written last, with no visible policy distinguishing the variants.
design_coherence.score_rationale: The structural foundation is sound — logger, builder, try-with-resources, and empty-collection idioms are uniformly applied. However three concrete divergences undercut developer trust: InvalidQuery is silently swallowed in one method and propagated in five others; two parallel nullable/Optional lookup paths exist for the same data; and SemanticFieldExplorer unconditionally closes an executor it may not own, creating a real double-close defect.

strengths: All genuine JUnit test files consistently use JUnit 5 Jupiter API with @Test, @DisplayName, @BeforeEach and Jupiter assertions throughout
Class naming (PascalCase), field naming (camelCase), constant naming (UPPER_SNAKE_CASE), and method naming are perfectly consistent across the entire source tree
Exception handling follows a clear project-wide pattern: try-with-resources for I/O, CQLParseException as the single domain unchecked exception
Javadoc coverage is excellent (~95% of public API methods documented) with consistent @param/@return/@throws usage
SLF4J is the single logging framework used across all classes that have logging, with a consistent static-field declaration pattern
convention_outlier.issue_character: The outliers cluster around test infrastructure hygiene (prefix vs suffix naming, non-JUnit files in the test tree) and one production class (BlackLabConllUIndexer) that bypasses the project-wide SLF4J pattern with raw System.out calls.
convention_outlier.score_rationale: The production source code is highly consistent in naming, structure, and patterns; the handful of deviations (package-private class, wildcard imports, missing logger) are minor. The test directory is a more significant source of confusion: three files either violate the *Test.java naming convention or masquerade as tests without any JUnit content. These issues are real but shallow and easily remedied, warranting a score in the upper-moderate range.

strengths: Builder pattern for WordSketchApiServer construction is clean and explicit
Consistent JSON response envelope (status, data fields) across most endpoints
HttpApiUtils centralises response serialization and query-param parsing
Error messages include descriptive text rather than bare codes
Endpoint URL design follows a consistent noun-hierarchy scheme (/api/sketch/{lemma}, /api/semantic-field/explore, /api/relations/dep)
api_surface_coherence.issue_character: Documentation drifted substantially from implementation, producing phantom endpoints and undocumented parameters, compounded by several response-structure inconsistencies and broken CORS on error paths.
api_surface_coherence.score_rationale: The documented API and the implemented API diverge in four concrete places (phantom endpoint, unimplemented query params, wrong highlighted/position fields, startup listing mismatch), which would block any integrator relying on the README. On top of that, the edge weight field is named differently across three related endpoints, and error responses skip the CORS header that success responses set, breaking browser clients.

strengths: CQLParserTest is extensive (30+ cases), covers real grammar macro patterns, edge cases like value-level vs field-level OR, and whitespace handling
LogDiceCalculatorTest goes beyond happy-path with symmetry and monotonicity property tests plus real-corpus regression values
LongIntHashMapTest properly exercises collision resolution by constructing the map with a deliberately small initial capacity
SimpleTaggerTest uses @BeforeEach for isolation and tests each linguistic rule (suffix, form, capitalization) individually
PredicatesTest and TokenPatternMatchTest test behavioral contracts cleanly with targeted positive and negative cases
test_strategy.issue_character: The pure-unit layer (parser, scoring, data structures) is tested with care, but the integration layer is represented by non-executable or always-skipping test shells that provide no CI protection.
test_strategy.score_rationale: Unit tests for algorithmic components (CQLParser, LogDice, LongIntHashMap, SimpleTagger, Predicates, TokenWindow) are solid and behaviorally oriented. However, several 'test' files are effectively dead code (a main() method, a no-assertion print loop), and the two most user-visible components — the REST API and SemanticFieldExplorer — have no tests at all. Integration tests for the BlackLab backend are permanently disabled via hardcoded local paths, eliminating any CI signal for the core query pipeline.

issues.summary: CLAUDE.md code-structure diagram lists ~10 classes that do not exist in the source tree
issues.suggestion: Rewrite the CLAUDE.md code-structure section to match the actual file tree. Remove phantom entries and add the real classes.
evidence: CLAUDE.md lists HybridLuceneIndexer.java, CollocationsBuilderV2.java, HybridIndex.java, HybridQueryExecutor.java, WordSketchQueryExecutor.java, SnowballCollocations.java, ConllUProcessor.java, CQLToLuceneCompiler.java under the code structure section.
None of these files exist under src/main/java/; the actual production classes are BlackLabQueryExecutor, BlackLabConllUIndexer, GrammarConfigLoader, SemanticFieldExplorer, and others.
CLAUDE.md also references 'SemanticFieldExplorer.java + SnowballCollocations.java' but SnowballCollocations.java does not exist.

issues.summary: WordSketchApiServer is a 1106-line God Class owning route registration, request parsing, business logic, and response serialisation for all endpoints
issues.suggestion: Extract each logical group of endpoints into its own handler class (e.g., SketchHandler, SemanticFieldHandler, ConcordanceHandler) that each accept a QueryExecutor and GrammarConfigLoader. WordSketchApiServer becomes a thin router registering context paths.
evidence: File is 1106 lines containing server.createContext() registrations for 10 routes, 12 private handleXxx methods each implementing full request→response cycles, a private RelationDef inner class, and a toLegacyRelationType() bridge method.
Any change to a relation, an endpoint, or the serialisation format of a single field requires navigating the entire file.

issues.summary: SketchGrammarParser is a full production class in the grammar package that is never called in production code
issues.suggestion: If superseded by GrammarConfigLoader, delete SketchGrammarParser and the .wsdef.m4 format support to remove the false capability signal.
evidence: grep for SketchGrammarParser in src/main/java finds only the class definition itself; no production class instantiates or imports it.
Runtime grammar loading is handled solely by GrammarConfigLoader reading JSON from the index directory.

issues.summary: Two .bak files committed inside src/main/java production source tree
issues.suggestion: Delete the .bak files and the empty hybrid/ package directory.
evidence: ls of src/main/java/.../indexer/hybrid/ shows only these two .bak files — no live .java files exist in that package.
.bak files in a Maven source tree signal uncommitted-work-in-progress.

issues.summary: Two parallel representations of grammatical relation types (QueryExecutor.RelationType enum vs GrammarConfigLoader string-based relationType) require a bridge method in the API server
issues.suggestion: Remove the RelationType enum from QueryExecutor. Let GrammarConfigLoader.RelationConfig be the single source of truth for relation patterns.
evidence: QueryExecutor.java defines RelationType enum with ADJ_MODIFIER, ADJ_PREDICATE, SUBJECT_OF, OBJECT_OF and hardcoded CQL patterns.
GrammarConfigLoader.RelationConfig carries a relationType() string field ('SURFACE', 'DEP') loaded from JSON.
WordSketchApiServer.toLegacyRelationType() bridges the two systems with a switch statement.

issues.summary: A full token-predicate evaluation subsystem (CQLVerifier, Token, TokenWindow, 6 predicate classes) lives in the query package but is never invoked on the production code path
issues.suggestion: If this subsystem is retained as a testing/verification utility, move it to src/test/java or a dedicated 'verification' sub-package. If superseded entirely by BlackLab, delete it.
evidence: Grep for CQLVerifier in src/main/java finds no caller in production code; only exercised by CQLVerifierTest and TokenPatternMatchTest.
BlackLabQueryExecutor delegates all query execution to BlackLab's CorpusQueryLanguageParser — it never constructs Token objects or calls TokenPredicate.test().

issues.summary: Token.matchesTag/matchesLemma/matchesWord/matchesPosGroup are never called; duplicate and diverge from AbstractPatternPredicate
issues.suggestion: Delete Token.matchesTag(), matchesLemma(), matchesWord(), matchesPosGroup(), and the private matchesPattern() from Token.java. Token is correctly used as a pure value object; all matching is properly delegated to the TokenPredicate hierarchy.
evidence: Token.matchesPattern() handles bracket character classes while AbstractPatternPredicate.wildcardToRegex() escapes them as literals — two incompatible wildcard semantics.
A full grep of src/main/java for .matchesTag(, .matchesLemma(, .matchesWord(, .matchesPosGroup( returns zero results.
CQLVerifier uses the AbstractPatternPredicate hierarchy exclusively — Token's matching API is parallel but inactive.

issues.summary: PosTagger interface has one implementation (SimpleTagger) that is always referenced by its concrete type, making the interface earn no indirection benefit
issues.suggestion: Either introduce the second tagger implementation the interface anticipates, or collapse SimpleTagger by removing the interface until a second implementation exists. If kept as an extension point, at minimum the factory methods should return PosTagger.
evidence: All call sites use SimpleTagger.create() or new SimpleTagger() and assign to SimpleTagger variables — no variable is ever declared as PosTagger.
CLAUDE.md lists UDPipeTagger as a component but it does not exist in the source tree.
SimpleTagger.create() returns SimpleTagger (not PosTagger), leaking the concrete type at the factory boundary.

issues.summary: QueryExecutor.RelationType embeds BCQL/CQL pattern strings inside the interface, leaking a concrete query language into the abstraction boundary
issues.suggestion: Move RelationType (with its CQL patterns) out of the QueryExecutor interface and into a BlackLab-specific or grammar-config layer. The interface should accept opaque pattern strings.
evidence: RelationType stores literal pattern strings like "[xpos=\"JJ.*\"] [lemma=\"%s\"]" — BlackLab CQL syntax baked into the interface shared by all implementors.
A hypothetical second QueryExecutor using a different pattern language cannot reuse RelationType without being forced to deal with CQL patterns it does not understand.

issues.summary: NotPredicate class duplicates TokenPredicate.negate() default method; neither is clearly canonical
issues.suggestion: Remove the negate() default from TokenPredicate (it is never called) and keep NotPredicate, which provides the useful toString() for debugging. Or keep negate() and replace new NotPredicate(pred) with pred.negate() throughout, then delete the class.
evidence: TokenPredicate declares a default negate() returning a lambda: token -> !this.test(token).
NotPredicate is a separate class doing the same thing with an added toString() method.
CQLVerifier line 192 uses new NotPredicate(pred) rather than pred.negate(), so the default method is never exercised in production.

issues.summary: exploreByPattern and explore implement an identical 4-step algorithm with 150+ lines copy-pasted
issues.suggestion: Extract a private scoreAndRankNouns(Map<String,Double> seedScores, Map<String,Map<String,Double>> nounProfiles, int minShared) method returning ExplorationResult. exploreByPattern and explore become thin wrappers that differ only in how they populate seedScores.
evidence: exploreByPattern (line 91) and explore (line 268) both: fetch collocates for seed, iterate to build nounProfiles, score nouns by sharedCount × avgLogDice, identify core collocates, sort by same comparator
Steps 3-4 share identical comparator lambda
The only variation is in Step 1 (how collocates are fetched) and the variable names (adj vs colloc)

issues.summary: splitByOrOutsideQuotes and splitByAnd in CQLParser are identical except for the delimiter character
issues.suggestion: Replace both with a single private List<String> splitOutsideQuotes(String s, char delimiter) method.
evidence: splitByOrOutsideQuotes and splitByAnd both iterate with inQuote toggle on '"', split on a delimiter, and collect tail via substring(start)
The only difference: delimiter is '|' in the first, '&' in the second

issues.summary: deriveHeadPositionFromPattern and deriveCollocatePositionFromPattern in GrammarConfigLoader are near-identical
issues.suggestion: Replace both with private static int derivePositionForLabel(String pattern, int label, int defaultPos) and call it with label=1 or label=2.
evidence: Both walk the pattern character by character, skip whitespace, check for N: prefixes, count tokens via '[' … ']' brackets
They differ only in which label digit triggers the return (1 vs 2) and the default return value

issues.summary: The 'group hits → compute logDice → build WordSketchResult list' pattern is duplicated four times in BlackLabQueryExecutor
issues.suggestion: Extract a private List<WordSketchResult> buildAndRankResults(Map<String,Long> freqMap, long headwordFreq, Map<String,String> posMap, double minLogDice, int maxResults) helper.
evidence: findCollocations, findDependencyCollocations, executeDependencyPattern, and executeSurfacePattern all end with: iterate freqMap entries, call LogDiceCalculator.compute, check >= minLogDice, add WordSketchResult, stream-sort-limit
The sorted-limit chain is literally copy-pasted four times

issues.summary: Lemma substitution code in CQLParser.buildElement extracts data and modifies target but the result is never used
issues.suggestion: Either complete the implementation or remove the entire block.
evidence: Lines 260-265: lemmaMatcher parses group(1) into subPos and group(2) into subField, then target = lemmaMatcher.replaceFirst('') modifies target, followed by comment '// Store lemma substitution info - will be used during compilation'
Neither subPos nor subField are stored anywhere; the modified target is passed to PatternElement but the substitution data is lost

issues.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer logs a Java Stream object reference instead of the list contents
issues.suggestion: Change to adjectives.stream().limit(5).map(r -> r.getLemma()).collect(Collectors.joining(', ')) or adjectives.subList(0, Math.min(5, adjectives.size())).
evidence: logger.info('  Top 5: {}', adjectives.stream()) — adjectives is already a List<WordSketchResult>; .stream() returns a Stream object whose toString is the internal descriptor

issues.summary: executeDependencyPattern executes the same BlackLab query twice unnecessarily
issues.suggestion: Execute the query once via blackLabIndex.search().find(query), call .execute() to materialize, then check groups.size() == 0 as the early-exit guard.
evidence: Line 380: Hits hits = blackLabIndex.find(query) — executed for null-guard (hits.size() == 0 check)
Line 391: SearchHits searchHits = blackLabIndex.search().find(query) — same query re-executed for grouping
The first Hits object is never used beyond the size check

issues.summary: executeBcqlQuery is a 130-line method mixing concordance fetching, collocate extraction, frequency accumulation, and logDice scoring
issues.suggestion: Extract a private HitRecord processHit(Hit, Concordances, int collocatePos) method and a private List<WordSketchResult> rankByLogDice(List<HitRecord>, ...) method.
evidence: Single method defines an inline record HitRecord, runs a first pass to extract collocates and build a frequency map, runs a second pass to compute logDice, then sorts and limits
The per-hit extraction logic accounts for ~40 lines inside the first-pass loop

issues.summary: GrammarConfigLoader constructor is a 120-line method mixing IO, validation, derivation, and indexing
issues.suggestion: Extract a private RelationConfig parseRelation(JSONObject relObj, int index) method and optionally a private void validateRoot(JSONObject root) method.
evidence: Constructor lines 52-170: reads file, validates version, rejects 'copulas' key, parses relations array, validates each relation's id/pattern/positions/token-count, builds two maps — all inline

issues.summary: Static constant REPITITION is a misspelling of REPETITION in CQLParser
issues.suggestion: Rename REPITITION to REPETITION and update the single reference on line 253.
evidence: Line 38: private static final Pattern REPITITION = Pattern.compile(...)
Line 253: Matcher repetitionMatcher = REPITITION.matcher(after) — the matcher variable is correctly named but the constant it references is misspelled

issues.summary: RelationDef inner static class in WordSketchApiServer is defined but never instantiated
issues.suggestion: Delete the RelationDef inner class entirely
evidence: Lines 1091-1106 define RelationDef with id/name/description/deprel/showInUi fields and constructor; no new RelationDef(...) call exists anywhere in the codebase

issues.summary: toLegacyRelationType() private method defined but never called
issues.suggestion: Delete the method; if the RelationType enum is also unused, delete that too
evidence: Lines 1071-1082 define toLegacyRelationType(); grep of the whole source tree finds no call site outside the definition

issues.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer.explore() duplicates exploreByPattern() logic (~150 lines) and is never called from outside the class
issues.suggestion: Delete explore(String,int,int,int,double) and explore(String)
evidence: explore(String,int,int,int,double) and explore(String) implement the same 4-step algorithm as exploreByPattern() with variable names changed; the API server always calls exploreByPattern(), never explore()

issues.summary: Identical relation-name-to-ID mapping switch is copy-pasted into handleSemanticFieldExplore and handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti
issues.suggestion: Extract to a private normalizeRelationId(String) method and call it from both handlers
evidence: The switch block mapping adj_modifier->noun_modifiers etc. appears verbatim at line ~500 and again at line ~650 with no extraction to a helper method

issues.summary: GrammarConfigLoader retains copulas/copulaSet fields and getCopulas()/isCopularVerb() methods that always return empty/false
issues.suggestion: Remove the copulas field, copulaSet field, getCopulas(), and isCopularVerb() methods
evidence: copulaSet is assigned Collections.emptySet() and never populated; isCopularVerb() always returns false; getCopulas() always returns empty list

issues.summary: GrammarConfigLoader.getRelationsForHeadPos(), findRelationIdByType(), and RelationConfig.isLegacyFormat() are defined but never called
issues.suggestion: Delete these three methods
evidence: grep of all .java files finds no call sites for these three methods outside their own definitions

issues.summary: LogDiceCalculator ships four extra scoring measures (MI3, TScore, LogLikelihood, FrequencyAggregator, CollocationResult) that are exercised only in tests and never used in production
issues.suggestion: If these measures are not planned for near-term use, remove them from LogDiceCalculator
evidence: computeMI3, computeTScore, computeLogLikelihood, FrequencyAggregator, CollocationResult appear only in LogDiceCalculator.java and LogDiceCalculatorTest.java; no production class imports or calls them

issues.summary: WordSketchApiServer uses a full 5-method Builder inner class for a 4-field object constructed exactly once
issues.suggestion: Replace with a plain constructor WordSketchApiServer(QueryExecutor, String, int, GrammarConfigLoader); delete the Builder inner class
evidence: Builder class with withExecutor/withIndexPath/withPort/withGrammarConfig/build methods; there is one call site in Main.java; no inheritance or optional-field benefit justifies the pattern

issues.summary: Token class duplicates pattern-matching logic already present in the AbstractPatternPredicate hierarchy
issues.suggestion: Delete the dead matching methods from Token, keeping it as a pure value object
evidence: Token.matchesTag/matchesLemma/matchesWord/matchesPosGroup each convert wildcard to regex — the exact same conversion logic is in AbstractPatternPredicate.wildcardToRegex; both systems coexist without cross-reference

issues.summary: 34 .bak files from the old hybrid/Lucene pipeline clutter the src/ tree and obscure the live architecture
issues.suggestion: Delete all .bak files from the source tree (git rm src/**/*.bak). The history is preserved in git if anyone needs to recover the old code.
evidence: find src/ -name '*.bak' returns 34 files spanning query/, indexer/hybrid/, grammar/, viz/, tools/, and test subtrees
git history would preserve these; there is no need for them as .bak files in the working tree

issues.summary: CLAUDE.md code-structure diagram and key-components section list six classes that no longer exist as .java files
issues.suggestion: Update CLAUDE.md to reflect the current BlackLab architecture: replace the code-structure diagram, remove references to deleted hybrid files, update the index-file section to describe BlackLab index structure.
evidence: CLAUDE.md line 103-113 lists CollocationsBuilderV2.java, HybridIndex.java, CQLToLuceneCompiler.java, SnowballCollocations.java, ConllUProcessor.java — all are .bak only
CLAUDE.md line 262 links to 'query/CQLToLuceneCompiler.java' (broken path)
CLAUDE.md lines 140, 182-184 describe collocations.bin, stats.bin, lexicon.bin index files that belong to the old hybrid index, not the BlackLab index

issues.summary: WordSketchApiServer.toLegacyRelationType() is defined but never called anywhere
issues.suggestion: Remove the toLegacyRelationType() method. If QueryExecutor.RelationType itself has no remaining callers, remove the enum too.
evidence: Method defined at line 1071 of WordSketchApiServer.java
grep for 'toLegacyRelationType' in src/ finds only the definition, no call sites

issues.summary: QueryExecutor.RelationType enum and findGrammaticalRelation() default method carry hardcoded Lucene-era CQL patterns never used by BlackLabQueryExecutor
issues.suggestion: Remove RelationType enum and findGrammaticalRelation() default method from QueryExecutor interface. Grammar-config-driven patterns via GrammarConfigLoader are the live mechanism.
evidence: RelationType.ADJ_MODIFIER has simplePattern '[xpos="JJ.*"]' — BlackLabQueryExecutor uses BCQL dependency syntax instead

findGrammaticalRelation() default implementation calls findCollocations(headword, relType.getSimplePattern(), ...) — BlackLabQueryExecutor never overrides this method and the API server never calls it

issues.summary: CQLParser, CQLPattern, CQLVerifier, and SketchGrammarParser are live production .java classes with no production callers
issues.suggestion: Move these classes to a test-only scope or delete them if there is no intention to revive the custom CQL engine.
evidence: No import of CQLParser, CQLVerifier, or SketchGrammarParser appears in any production src/main file outside of their own definitions
CQLVerifier is exercised only in CQLVerifierTest (test code) and CQLParserTest uses CQLParser
These were part of the custom Lucene pattern matching pipeline replaced by BlackLab's BCQL parser

issues.summary: GrammarConfigLoader.RelationConfig.isLegacyFormat() is defined but never called
issues.suggestion: Remove isLegacyFormat() from RelationConfig.
evidence: Method defined in GrammarConfigLoader.java; grep for 'isLegacyFormat' across all .java files returns only the definition

issues.summary: QueryResults.java Javadoc still refers to the deleted WordSketchQueryExecutor class
issues.suggestion: Update the Javadoc to remove the WordSketchQueryExecutor reference and describe the actual current purpose.
evidence: Line 7: '* Extracted from WordSketchQueryExecutor for reuse across different executor implementations.'
WordSketchQueryExecutor.java exists only as a .bak file

issues.summary: query package is a catch-all blob mixing token model, predicate hierarchy, executors, result DTOs, and exploration logic
issues.suggestion: Extract at least three sub-packages: query.model (Token, TokenWindow, QueryResults), query.predicate (predicate hierarchy), and query.explore (SemanticFieldExplorer). Alternatively move token model to a top-level model package shared across query and grammar packages.
evidence: 19 classes totalling 2682 lines in one package, spanning at least four coherent sub-domains
Token + TokenWindow are corpus domain model, not query execution
9-class predicate hierarchy (TokenPredicate, AbstractPatternPredicate, Lemma/Tag/Word/PosGroup/And/Or/Not Predicate) is a self-contained combinator library for CQL evaluation
SemanticFieldExplorer (854 lines) is a high-level exploration algorithm, not a query primitive

issues.summary: LongIntHashMapTest placed in query test package instead of utils test package
issues.suggestion: Move LongIntHashMapTest to src/test/java/pl/marcinmilkowski/word_sketch/utils/ and update its package declaration to pl.marcinmilkowski.word_sketch.utils.
evidence: LongIntHashMapTest declares package pl.marcinmilkowski.word_sketch.query
The class under test, LongIntHashMap, is in pl.marcinmilkowski.word_sketch.utils
LogDiceCalculatorTest correctly mirrors its source in pl.marcinmilkowski.word_sketch.utils

issues.summary: RelationType enum (grammatical relations) is nested inside QueryExecutor infrastructure interface
issues.suggestion: Extract RelationType to its own top-level class in the grammar package (e.g., grammar.GrammaticalRelation) where it co-locates with other linguistic pattern definitions.
evidence: RelationType enum with values ADJ_MODIFIER, ADJ_PREDICATE, SUBJECT_OF, OBJECT_OF is a static nested type inside QueryExecutor
It carries CQL patterns and represents grammatical/linguistic domain knowledge, not query infrastructure
The grammar package already holds domain-level CQL concepts (CQLParser, CQLPattern)

issues.summary: utils package is a catch-all pairing an unrelated domain statistic class with a generic data structure
issues.suggestion: Rename/replace utils: move LogDiceCalculator to a statistics or scoring package; move LongIntHashMap to an infrastructure or collections package, or inline it where used if only used in one place.
evidence: LogDiceCalculator is a corpus-statistics domain class with five distinct association measures and inner result/aggregator classes (183 lines of domain logic)
LongIntHashMap is a generic performance-optimised data structure with no domain dependency
They share a package only because neither fit elsewhere at the time of creation

issues.summary: InvalidQuery swallowed silently in findCollocations but propagated as IOException in five other methods
issues.suggestion: Decide on one policy: either always propagate InvalidQuery wrapped as IOException, or always log-and-return-empty. Add a brief comment if an intentional exception applies to findCollocations.
evidence: findCollocations() catch(InvalidQuery e): logger.warn(...); return Collections.emptyList()
findDependencyCollocations() catch(InvalidQuery e): throw new IOException("CQL parse error: " + e.getMessage(), e)
executeQuery(), executeBcqlQuery(), executeSurfacePattern() all re-throw as IOException

issues.summary: GrammarConfigLoader exposes both getRelation() returning Optional and getRelationById() returning nullable for the same lookup
issues.suggestion: Remove getRelationById() and standardise all callers on getRelation() returning Optional.
evidence: GrammarConfigLoader line 313: public Optional<RelationConfig> getRelation(String id)
GrammarConfigLoader line 320: public RelationConfig getRelationById(String id) { return relationsById.get(id); }  // returns null if missing
WordSketchApiServer uses getRelationById at lines 344 and 447; getRelation at lines 514, 666, and 898

issues.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer.close() unconditionally closes an injected executor it does not own, causing double-close at server shutdown
issues.suggestion: Add a boolean ownsExecutor field set to true only when the indexPath constructor is used, false when the executor-injection constructor is used. In close(), only close executor when ownsExecutor is true.
evidence: SemanticFieldExplorer(QueryExecutor executor) constructor: this.executor = executor — does not set ownership flag
SemanticFieldExplorer.close(): always closes executor if AutoCloseable
WordSketchApiServer calls new SemanticFieldExplorer(this.executor) then explorer.close() at lines 541, 783, 861
Main.java shutdown hook also calls executor.close() — BlackLabIndex closed twice

issues.summary: logger.debug() calls embed literal 'DEBUG:' text prefix in message strings, which is redundant and inconsistent with standard logging idiom
issues.suggestion: Remove the 'DEBUG:' and 'DEBUG ' string prefixes from all logger.debug() message strings.
evidence: WordSketchApiServer: logger.debug("DEBUG: After getFullPattern: {}", ...)
WordSketchApiServer: logger.debug("DEBUG Radial: body = {}", body)
PatternSubstitution: logger.debug("DEBUG: substituteCollocate: ...")

issues.summary: RelationDef private inner class in WordSketchApiServer is defined but never instantiated or referenced
issues.suggestion: Delete the RelationDef inner class.
evidence: Lines 1091-1104: private static class RelationDef with id, name, description, deprel, showInUi fields and constructor
No new RelationDef(...) expression anywhere in the file or codebase
GrammarConfigLoader.RelationConfig record serves the same purpose

issues.summary: Two test files use 'Test*' prefix instead of the project-wide '*Test' suffix convention
issues.suggestion: Rename TestBCQLParser.java to BCQLParserTest.java and rename TestGrammarConfig.java to GrammarConfigTestHelper.java to align with the *Test suffix convention and distinguish helpers from executable tests.
evidence: 12 other test files follow *Test.java: CQLParserTest.java, LogDiceCalculatorTest.java, SimpleTaggerTest.java, etc.
TestBCQLParser.java contains real @Test methods but is named with 'Test' as a prefix
TestGrammarConfig.java is a static factory/helper with no @Test methods at all

issues.summary: RelationsValidationTest.java is a standalone script with main() and no JUnit annotations, misleadingly named as a test
issues.suggestion: Either convert it to a proper JUnit 5 test (replace main() with @Test void validateRelationsHavePatterns() using assertions) or rename it to RelationsValidationScript.java and move it to a tools source set.
evidence: File has no import of org.junit or any test framework
Contains only 'public static void main(String[] args) throws IOException' — runs as a CLI tool
Named *Test.java so it appears in test listings alongside real JUnit tests but is never executed by Maven Surefire

issues.summary: BlackLabConllUIndexer uses System.out/System.err instead of SLF4J, unlike every other non-trivial class
issues.suggestion: Add 'private static final Logger logger = LoggerFactory.getLogger(BlackLabConllUIndexer.class)' and replace System.out calls with logger.info() and System.err calls with logger.error().
evidence: Lines 40, 47, 58, 94, 111-114: 10+ direct System.out.println / System.err.println calls
No SLF4J import and no 'private static final Logger logger' field in the class
All other complex classes declare 'private static final Logger logger = LoggerFactory.getLogger(ClassName.class)'

issues.summary: BlackLabSnippetParser has no public visibility modifier, making it package-private unlike all other main-source classes
issues.suggestion: Either add a short comment ('// package-private: internal parsing detail of BlackLabQueryExecutor') to make the restriction explicit, or make it public to match the rest of the codebase.
evidence: Class declaration reads 'class BlackLabSnippetParser' (no public keyword)
All other classes in src/main are declared 'public class ...'
The class is only consumed within the query package

issues.summary: Seven source files use 'import java.util.*' wildcard while the majority use fully-qualified specific imports
issues.suggestion: Add a Checkstyle rule (AvoidStarImport) to pom.xml to enforce specific imports project-wide, then expand the wildcards in the 7 offending files.
evidence: grep for 'import java.util.*' finds 7 files with wildcard; remaining 24 source files use specific imports
No checkstyle or import ordering enforcement present in pom.xml

issues.summary: README documents /api/grammar/active which is never registered in the server
issues.suggestion: Either register the endpoint or remove the reference from README
evidence: README: curl http://localhost:8080/api/grammar/active
No server.createContext("/api/grammar/active", ...) call exists in WordSketchApiServer.java

issues.summary: README documents ?pos, ?limit, and ?scoring params for /api/sketch/{lemma} but server ignores them all
issues.suggestion: Parse and honour pos, limit, min_logdice, and scoring params in handleFullSketch(), or remove them from README examples
evidence: README: curl "http://localhost:8080/api/sketch/house?pos=noun,verb&limit=15" and ?scoring=logDice variants
handleFullSketch() does not call parseQueryParams() at all; hardcodes minLogDice=0.0 and limit=20

issues.summary: Concordance response documented with HTML highlighting and position arrays but code returns plain text and omits position fields
issues.suggestion: Implement actual highlighting by scanning token positions and building <mark>-tagged HTML; add position arrays, or update README to reflect actual response shape
evidence: README documents highlighted field with <mark> tags and word1_positions/word2_positions arrays
handleConcordanceExamples(): exMap.put("highlighted", r.getSentence()) — no mark tags
No word1_positions or word2_positions keys are ever added to exMap

issues.summary: sendError() omits Access-Control-Allow-Origin header, breaking browser CORS for all error responses
issues.suggestion: Add exchange.getResponseHeaders().set("Access-Control-Allow-Origin", "*") inside sendError() before sendResponseHeaders()
evidence: sendJsonResponse() line 24: exchange.getResponseHeaders().set("Access-Control-Allow-Origin", "*")
sendError() has no Access-Control-Allow-Origin header — any 4xx/5xx response will be opaque to browser clients

issues.summary: BCQL error catch block returns HTTP 200 with {"error":...} instead of an error status code
issues.suggestion: Replace sendJsonResponse with sendError(exchange, 500, e.getMessage()) in the catch block
evidence: handleBcqlQueryPost() catch block: calls HttpApiUtils.sendJsonResponse(exchange, errorResponse) which always sends 200
All other handlers use HttpApiUtils.sendError(exchange, 500, ...) for exceptions

issues.summary: Edge objects use 'logDice' key in /api/semantic-field but 'weight' in both explore endpoints
issues.suggestion: Standardise on one field name (preferably 'logDice' since that is the semantic meaning) for edge association scores across all three endpoints
evidence: handleSemanticField(): edgeMap.put("logDice", Math.round(edge.weight * 100.0) / 100.0)
handleSemanticFieldExplore(): edgeMap.put("weight", Math.round(edge.weight * 100.0) / 100.0)
handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti(): edgeMap.put("weight", wsr.getLogDice())

issues.summary: README startup-output example lists only 4 endpoints; the server actually registers 11+
issues.suggestion: Update the README startup snippet to match the logger.info lines already present in WordSketchApiServer.start()
evidence: README shows startup listing only /health, /api/sketch/{lemma}, /api/semantic-field/explore, /api/semantic-field/explore-multi
Server start() registers 10+ endpoints including /api/relations, /api/relations/dep, /api/semantic-field, /api/concordance/examples, /api/visual/radial, /api/bcql

issues.summary: Legacy relation alias switch is duplicated verbatim in handleSemanticFieldExplore and handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti
issues.suggestion: Extract the mapping into a private static helper method resolveRelationId(String) and call it from both handlers
evidence: Lines 501-507: switch block mapping adj_modifier->noun_modifiers etc. in handleSemanticFieldExplore
Lines 653-659: identical switch block in handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti

issues.summary: BlackLabDummyTest has no assertions and silently swallows all exceptions
issues.suggestion: Either convert to a real parameterized test with assertions on result sizes/content, or delete it. If the intent is exploratory debugging, move it to a separate manual test source set excluded from `mvn test`.
evidence: Method body checks `if (!indexDir.exists()) { return; }` — returns silently instead of skipping via Assumptions
Loop catches all exceptions with `System.out.println("ERROR: " + e.getMessage())` — failures are invisible
No `assert*` or `fail()` calls anywhere in the test

issues.summary: RelationsValidationTest uses main() not @Test — Maven Surefire never runs it
issues.suggestion: Convert to a proper JUnit 5 test class: annotate the validation logic with `@Test` and replace `System.exit(1)` with `assertEquals(0, withoutPattern, "Relations missing patterns: " + ...)`.
evidence: Class declares `public static void main(String[] args)` with no JUnit annotations
No `@Test` method present; the class will be compiled but never executed by Maven Surefire

issues.summary: DependencySketchTest and BlackLabBcqlConcordanceTest always skip in CI due to hardcoded local Windows paths
issues.suggestion: Extract the pure-logic edge-case tests (empty lemma, null deprel) into unit tests that mock or stub the executor. For real index tests, read the path from an environment variable or system property (`INDEX_PATH` env var with `Assumptions.assumeTrue(path != null)`).
evidence: DependencySketchTest: `private static final String INDEX_PATH = "D:\\corpora_philsci\\bi";` with `Assumptions.assumeTrue(indexDir.exists(), ...)`
BlackLabBcqlConcordanceTest: same path, `@EnabledIf("indexExists")` where `indexExists()` checks the hardcoded path
The `testDependencyEmptyLemma` and `testDependencyNullDeprel` tests verify pure logic (empty/null input) but still depend on the external index to run

issues.summary: WordSketchApiServer, SemanticFieldExplorer, and BlackLabSnippetParser have zero test coverage
issues.suggestion: Add unit tests for BlackLabSnippetParser using hardcoded XML fixture strings. Add at least one integration smoke test for the /api/sketch/{lemma} endpoint using an embedded HTTP server. SemanticFieldExplorer can be tested with a mock QueryExecutor returning fixture collocations.
evidence: No test file found matching WordSketchApiServer or SemanticFieldExplorer
CLAUDE.md lists SemanticFieldExplorer as a key component with single-seed exploration logic
BlackLabSnippetParser parses XML snippets from BlackLab — string parsing logic that is easily unit-testable with fixture strings

issues.summary: CQLVerifierTest verifies only isMatched() boolean; matched position and collocate identity are never asserted
issues.suggestion: Extend assertions to also check the matched token's lemma/position from `VerificationResult`. For `testNounPattern`, assert the matched token is NOT the headword (position 2) to confirm the verifier excludes the head from collocate candidates.
evidence: 13 out of 15 test methods only call `assertTrue(result.isMatched(), ...)` or `assertFalse(result.isMatched())`
`testMultiElementPattern` comment says "Should find both 'big' (jj) at position 1 and 'is' (vb) at position 3" but only checks the boolean result
No test verifies `result.getMatchedCollocate()`, matched position, or that the correct token was selected when multiple tokens match
